report_name: ‘FortiBleed’ Campaign Harvests Credentials from 86,000+ Fortinet Devices

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: The FortiBleed campaign used large lists of common passwords and credentials stolen from previous data breaches to launch password spraying and brute-force attacks against Fortinet devices.

attack_patterns.name: Password Guessing
attack_patterns.description: The FortiBleed campaign used password guessing with common and default passwords against user accounts on exposed Fortinet management interfaces.

attack_patterns.name: Password Spraying
attack_patterns.description: The FortiBleed campaign used a small list of common passwords against a large list of usernames to compromise Fortinet devices and avoid account lockouts.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers leveraged credentials stolen from other data breaches to gain access to Fortinet devices, exploiting password reuse as part of the FortiBleed campaign.

attack_patterns.name: Network Device CLI
attack_patterns.description: Attackers may use diagnostic commands such as 'diagnose debug' on a compromised FortiGate device to gather system information and explore the device's configuration.

campaigns.name: FortiBleed
campaigns.description: A massive, ongoing credential-harvesting campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate firewalls and SSL VPN gateways globally. The campaign uses automated scanning, brute-force, and password spraying to compromise devices with weak credentials.
campaigns.objective: Harvest credentials from Fortinet devices and VPN traffic.

infrastructure.name: FortiBleed Scanning and Brute-Force Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Automated infrastructure used to scan the internet for exposed Fortinet devices and launch brute-force/password-spraying attacks.

indicators.name: FortiGate Debug Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: FortiGate Debug Command Execution
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e4f-4b2a-9c1d-6a3b8d7e4f1a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'diagnose debug' commands on a FortiGate device. This may indicate an attacker performing reconnaissance on a compromised device.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/__sentry?ctype=balanced&uri=/vulnerabilities/global-cybersecurity-agencies-warn-of-credential-exposure-in-fortibleed-campaign-targeting-fortinet-firewalls-vpn-gateways/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-24
tags:
  - attack.t1059.008
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: command
  product: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    command|startswith: 'diagnose debug'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'diagnose debug' commands on a FortiGate device, which may indicate an attacker performing reconnaissance on a compromised device as part of the FortiBleed campaign.

indicators.name: FortiGate SSL VPN Login Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: FortiGate SSL VPN Login Attempt
id: a8d7e6f5-4c3b-4a2a-8b1c-9d0e1f2a3b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects login attempts to FortiGate SSL VPN portals. A high volume of attempts from a single source IP may indicate brute-forcing as seen in the FortiBleed campaign.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/__sentry?ctype=balanced&uri=/vulnerabilities/global-cybersecurity-agencies-warn-of-credential-exposure-in-fortibleed-campaign-targeting-fortinet-firewalls-vpn-gateways/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-24
tags:
  - attack.t1110
  - attack.credential_access
level: informational
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem: '/remote/login'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects login attempts to FortiGate SSL VPN portals. While a single event is informational, a high volume may indicate brute-force activity related to the FortiBleed campaign.
